examples.title: Linear Search Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates O(n) time complexity with best, worst, and average cases
examples.explanation: Linear search examines each element sequentially until target is found. The loop runs at most n times, giving linear time complexity.

examples.title: Binary Search Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates O(log n) time complexity for searching in sorted arrays
examples.code:
FUNCTION BinarySearch(arr[1...n], target)
  left ← 1
  right ← n
  
  WHILE left ≤ right DO
    mid ← (left + right) / 2
    
    IF arr[mid] = target THEN
      RETURN mid
    ELSE IF arr[mid] < target THEN
      left ← mid + 1
    ELSE
      right ← mid - 1
    END IF
  END WHILE
  
  RETURN -1  // Not found
END FUNCTION

// Recurrence: T(n) = T(n/2) + O(1)
// By Master Theorem: T(n) = O(log n)
examples.explanation: Binary search repeatedly divides the search space in half. With each iteration, the range [left, right] reduces by approximately half until the target is found or the range becomes empty.

examples.title: Merge Sort Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates Θ(n log n) time complexity with O(n) space
examples.code:
def merge_sort(arr):
    if len(arr) <= 1:
        return arr
    
    # Divide
    mid = len(arr) // 2
    left = merge_sort(arr[:mid])
    right = merge_sort(arr[mid:])
    
    # Conquer (merge)
    return merge(left, right)

def merge(left, right):
    result = []
    i = j = 0
    
    while i < len(left) and j < len(right):
        if left[i] <= right[j]:
            result.append(left[i])
            i += 1
        else:
            result.append(right[j])
            j += 1
    
    result.extend(left[i:])
    result.extend(right[j:])
    return result

# Time Complexity: T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n) = Θ(n log n)
# Space Complexity: O(n) for auxiliary array
examples.explanation: Merge sort divides the array into halves recursively (log n levels), then merges them in O(n) time at each level. Total: n × log n = n log n operations.

examples.title: Nested Loop Analysis Example
examples.description: Demonstrates O(n³) time complexity from triple nested loops
examples.explanation: Three nested loops each running n times results in n³ total iterations. This is cubic time complexity, which grows rapidly with input size and is inefficient for large n.
